chapter: Chapter: Weavers, Iron Smelters and Factory Owners

questions.question: Who were weavers?
questions.explanation: Weavers were people who made cloth using yarn.

questions.question: Which material did Indian weavers mainly produce?
questions.explanation: Indian weavers were famous for producing cotton and silk cloth.

questions.question: Indian textiles were famous in which places?
questions.explanation: Indian textiles were exported and admired across the world.

questions.question: Which tool was commonly used by weavers?
questions.explanation: Weavers used looms to weave yarn into cloth.

questions.question: Who were iron smelters?

options.A: People who produced iron from ore

questions.explanation: Iron smelters produced iron by heating iron ore.

questions.question: What was iron mainly used for?
questions.explanation: Iron was used to make tools, weapons and farming equipment.

questions.question: Which industry developed during British rule in India?
questions.explanation: Factory industries started growing during British rule.

questions.question: Who owned factories?
questions.explanation: Factories were owned by industrialists called factory owners.

questions.question: Which type of cloth was famous in India?
questions.explanation: Indian cotton cloth was famous worldwide.

questions.question: Which machine helped produce cloth in factories?
questions.explanation: Power looms were machines used in textile factories.

questions.question: Where did traditional weavers usually work?
questions.explanation: Traditional weavers usually worked from their homes.

questions.question: Which country introduced machine-made cloth to India?
questions.explanation: Machine-made cloth from Britain affected Indian weavers.

questions.question: What happened to many Indian weavers during British rule?
questions.explanation: Machine-made cloth from Britain caused many problems for Indian weavers.

questions.question: Which natural resource was used by iron smelters?
questions.explanation: Iron smelters used iron ore to produce iron.

questions.question: Which place became famous for textile factories in India?
questions.explanation: Bombay became an important centre for textile mills.

questions.question: What did factory machines help increase?
questions.explanation: Machines helped increase the production of cloth.

questions.question: Which group made cloth using handlooms?
questions.explanation: Weavers produced cloth using handlooms.

questions.question: Which industry declined due to factory production?
questions.explanation: Handloom weaving declined due to machine-made cloth.

questions.question: Which material did Indian weavers commonly use?
questions.explanation: Cotton was widely used by Indian weavers.

questions.question: Who worked in factories producing cloth?
questions.explanation: Factory workers worked in textile factories producing cloth.

questions.question: Which method produced cloth before factories?
questions.explanation: Before factories, cloth was produced using handlooms.

questions.question: Which city was an early textile mill center in India?
questions.explanation: Bombay developed as an important textile mill center.

questions.question: Which type of cloth was exported from India historically?
questions.explanation: Indian cotton textiles were exported around the world.

questions.question: Which activity did iron smelters perform?
questions.explanation: Iron smelters produced iron by extracting it from iron ore.

questions.question: Which change affected traditional weavers the most?
questions.explanation: Machine-made cloth from factories greatly affected traditional weavers.
